suggested_topics: why some bridges last 2000 years and others collapse in 20
how coral reefs build their own weather systems
the logistics of running a 24-hour diner
why birds migrate in V formations — physics not instinct
the hidden math behind musical scales

formats.name: micro_diary
formats.instruction: Jot down a single fleeting moment from your day — use poetic language, present tense, and end with an unexpected twist. 1-2 sentences only.

formats.name: imaginary_dialogue
formats.instruction: Invent a short conversation between two unlikely characters (e.g. your phone and your houseplant) about today. Make it quirky and lighthearted. 70-120 words.

formats.name: secret_recipe
formats.instruction: Describe the 'secret recipe' to achieving something unusual (e.g. happiness, finding lost socks) as if it's a dish. List out ingredients and playful instructions. 60-90 words.

formats.name: hot_take_list
formats.instruction: Share your boldest opinions about a topic in 4-5 bullet points. No explanations, just snappy statements. Entire post must not exceed 70 words.

formats.name: time_machine_letter
formats.instruction: Write a letter to your past/future self (pick an age!), giving advice or asking questions. Use direct address and strong emotion. 150-300 words.

formats.name: out_of_context_fact
formats.instruction: Drop a fascinating fact about the world, but frame it in a way that makes it sound mysterious or cryptic. One sentence only.

formats.name: theme_park_blueprint
formats.instruction: Invent a new theme park attraction based on a real-life experience. Describe its rules, features, and the twist at the end. 200-350 words.

formats.name: contrarian_story
formats.instruction: Tell a story about a time you went against the grain or broke a norm. Make your stance clear and explain your reasoning. 100-250 words.

formats.name: color_palette_review
formats.instruction: Pick a mood or concept (e.g. Monday, nostalgia) and assign it a palette of colors. Explain why you chose each shade. 60-100 words.

title_styles: Write a surreal, whimsical title. Examples: 'A conversation between my alarm clock and my willpower', 'If socks had a secret society'
Write a suspenseful headline that teases a mystery. Examples: 'Why my morning started with an orange light', 'The hidden ingredient in every Tuesday'
Write an instructive title that promises an odd lesson. Examples: 'How to decode your neighbor’s wifi names', 'The unofficial guide to surviving awkward elevator rides'
Write a nostalgic title that references a past era or childhood memory. Examples: 'A letter to the kid who hated broccoli', 'The lost art of mixtape-making'
Write a provocative, challenge-based title. Examples: 'Break one social rule: Here’s what happened', 'Defending the indefensible: My case for pineapple on pizza'
structure_variants: Start with a vivid sensory detail, then spiral outward to the main idea.
Sandwich your argument between two unexpected metaphors.
Use an alternating structure: one fact, one emotion, one fact, one emotion.
Conclude with a mini-script or imagined scene related to your topic.
Present your story in reverse — begin at the ending, unpack how you got there.
topic_angles: Rituals and habits nobody talks about
The secret history behind mundane objects
How technology accidentally changed your daily life
Hidden connections between feelings and furniture
